content.claim: Rural settlements in Western Transdanubia, Southern Baranya, and low-density regions of Northern Hungary show child population contractions of 10-30% over recent periods, with some settlements reporting only 50-70 school-age children total, creating structural viability challenges for multi-track (4/6/8-year) selective pipelines independent of national cohort decline.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Specific time period and comparison baseline not detailed in notes
Relationship between child population size and actual school closure/downgrading decisions not quantified
Regional variation in institutional resilience and alternative provision models not examined
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
